channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: Feasts of the Saints:August 29
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: Feasts of the Saints:August 29

item.title: Feasts of the Saints, 2008-09-11
item.description: :Feasts of the Saints. Thoughts concering the Kontakion of the feast of the beheading of the Forerunner, and the precious gift of clarity of thought sometimes given especially during the early morning liturgy.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Feasts of the Saints. Thoughts concering the Kontakion of the feast of the beheading of the Forerunner, and the precious gift of clarity of thought sometimes given especially during the early morning liturgy.

item.title: Feasts of the Saints, Beheading Of The Glorious Prophet Forerunner And Baptist John, Hidden Addictions And Passions Kill., 2011-09-11
item.description: :Feasts of the Saints. The story of the beheading of John the Baptist shows us how an ordinary life ends in death. Herod was an ordinary man, since he was ruled by his passions and sins (lust,adultery,ambition,drunkeness) and because of his indulgence in his passions, most if which he was not aware of, he was also a coward, and when he made an unwise oath, he was unwilling to renege on it to save face. Therefore, he had Saint john beheaded, who then passed into eternal life, and Herod continued his descent into eternal death. We explore especially how hidden passions (present in all of us!) can kill the soul.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Feasts of the Saints. The story of the beheading of John the Baptist shows us how an ordinary life ends in death. Herod was an ordinary man, since he was ruled by his passions and sins (lust,adultery,ambition,drunkeness) and because of his indulgence in his passions, most if which he was not aware of, he was also a coward, and when he made an unwise oath, he was unwilling to renege on it to save face. Therefore, he had Saint john beheaded, who then passed into eternal life, and Herod continued his descent into eternal death. We explore especially how hidden passions (present in all of us!) can kill the soul.

item.title: Feasts of the Saints, Beheading of the Glorious Prophet Forerunner and Baptist John, 2014-09-10
item.description: :Feasts of the Saints. Beheading of the Glorious Prophet Forerunner and Baptist John. Why did Herod behead St John, even though he loved him? His actions are a case study in how immoral life kills the soul in every way.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Feasts of the Saints. Beheading of the Glorious Prophet Forerunner and Baptist John. Why did Herod behead St John, even though he loved him? His actions are a case study in how immoral life kills the soul in every way.

item.title: Feasts of the Saints, Beheading of the Glorious Prophet Forerunner and Baptist John, 2014-09-11
item.description: :Feasts of the Saints. Beheading of the Glorious Prophet Forerunner and Baptist John. Why remember the Saints? They teach us to stop feeling entightled that everybody would be nice to us and things would go smoothly.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Feasts of the Saints. Beheading of the Glorious Prophet Forerunner and Baptist John. Why remember the Saints? They teach us to stop feeling entightled that everybody would be nice to us and things would go smoothly.
